name: National Human Trafficking Hotline
description: 24/7 confidential hotline for trafficking survivors and concerned individuals. Provides crisis intervention, safety planning, and referrals to local services in over 200 languages.

name: Polaris Project Resource Directory
description: National anti-trafficking organization maintaining a directory of vetted local service providers including shelters, legal aid, and medical care.

name: T Visa Legal Assistance Network
description: Nonprofit legal aid organizations specializing in T-visa applications for trafficking survivors, providing free or low-cost representation.

name: U Visa Legal Assistance Network
description: Legal aid network for survivors of qualifying crimes (including trafficking) seeking U nonimmigrant status, offering case evaluation and representation.

name: HHS Office on Trafficking in Persons (OTIP)
description: Federal office coordinating anti-trafficking response, including the Certification process that allows survivors to access federal benefits regardless of immigration status.

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
description: 24/7 hotline supporting survivors of domestic violence and related exploitation, including safety planning and emergency shelter referrals.

name: RAINN (Rape, Abuse & Incest National Network)
description: Operates the National Sexual Assault Hotline and connects survivors to local medical, counseling, and advocacy resources.

name: Local Trafficking Survivor Shelter Network
description: Placeholder entry representing state/regional trafficking-specific shelter networks. In production this would be populated per-jurisdiction from vetted NGO partner data, not scraped.

name: Immigrant Legal Resource Center
description: Provides know-your-rights materials and legal referrals for immigrants, including trafficking and crime victims navigating immigration status.

name: Coalition to Abolish Slavery and Trafficking (CAST)
description: California-based comprehensive service provider offering case management, shelter, legal services, and job training for trafficking survivors.
